The '''Wetakk''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of serpentine build with six [[Limb/Legends|arms]].<ref name="TAS" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'' to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> a [[Unidentified Wetakk|Wetakk]] was present in [[Cuipernam]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ansion/Legends|Ansion]]. It was part of a multi-species group hired by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Soergg Vosadii Bezhin]], to attack the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] and her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], and derail their attempt to keep Ansion as a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Attacking Offee with a hooked [[Melee weapon/Legends|blade]], the Wetakk had one of its arms severed. While the attacking group almost got the better of the two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], the arrival of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] tipped the balance towards the Jedi [[Gender/Legends|woman]], and the Wetakk's group eventually fled. Despite this attempt on their lives, the Jedi succeeded in negotiating with Ansion's representatives to stay part of the Republic.<ref name="TAS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Wetakk first appeared in ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'', a [[2002]] novel written by [[Alan Dean Foster]].<ref name="TAS" />
